formulas.name: The Law of Universal Gravitation
formulas.variables: F: Force, G: Gravitational constant, m1: Mass of object 1, m2: Mass of object 2, r: Distance between objects
formulas.units: N (Newtons) for force, m^3 kg^-1 s^-2 for gravitational constant
formulas.notes: This law explains the gravitational force between two objects and is used to describe the motion of planets around the sun.

formulas.name: Hooke's Law
formulas.variables: F: Force, k: Spring constant, x: Displacement from equilibrium position
formulas.units: N (Newtons) for force, Nm^-1 (Newtons per meter) for spring constant
formulas.notes: This law describes the relationship between the force exerted on a spring and its displacement from its equilibrium position.

formulas.name: Work-Energy Theorem
formulas.variables: W: Work done, ΔE: Change in energy
formulas.notes: This theorem states that the work done on an object is equal to the change in its energy.

formulas.name: Energy Conservation
formulas.variables: E: Total energy, E_initial: Initial energy, W: Work done
formulas.notes: This law states that the total energy of an isolated system remains constant over time.

formulas.name: Power
formulas.variables: P: Power, W: Work done, t: Time taken
formulas.units: W (Watts) for power, J (Joules) for work and time in seconds
formulas.notes: This formula calculates the rate at which work is done.

formulas.name: Impulse-Momentum Theorem
formulas.variables: J: Impulse, Δp: Change in momentum
formulas.units: Ns (Newtons seconds) for impulse and momentum
formulas.notes: This theorem states that the impulse applied to an object is equal to the change in its momentum.

formulas.name: Momentum
formulas.variables: p: Momentum, m: Mass, v: Velocity
formulas.units: kg m/s (kilogram meters per second) for momentum, kg for mass, m/s for velocity
formulas.notes: This formula calculates the momentum of an object.

formulas.name: Velocity-Time Graph
formulas.variables: v: Velocity, Δx: Displacement, Δt: Time taken
formulas.units: m/s (meters per second) for velocity, m for displacement and time in seconds
formulas.notes: This formula calculates the average velocity of an object from a velocity-time graph.

formulas.name: Displacement-Time Graph
formulas.notes: This formula calculates the displacement of an object from a displacement-time graph.

formulas.name: Acceleration-Time Graph
formulas.variables: a: Acceleration, Δv: Change in velocity, Δt: Time taken
formulas.units: m/s^2 (meters per second squared) for acceleration, m/s for velocity and time in seconds
formulas.notes: This formula calculates the average acceleration of an object from an acceleration-time graph.

formulas.name: Velocity-Displacement Graph
formulas.variables: v: Velocity, Δx: Displacement, Δt: Time taken
formulas.units: m/s (meters per second) for velocity, m for displacement and time in seconds
formulas.notes: This formula calculates the average velocity of an object from a velocity-displacement graph.

formulas.name: Area Under Velocity-Time Graph
formulas.variables: s: Displacement, v: Velocity, t: Time
formulas.units: m (meters) for displacement, m/s (meters per second) for velocity and time in seconds
formulas.notes: This formula calculates the displacement of an object from the area under its velocity-time graph.

formulas.name: Area Under Displacement-Time Graph
formulas.units: m (meters) for displacement and time in seconds
formulas.notes: This formula calculates the displacement of an object from the area under its displacement-time graph.
